title: Mobility-support stair evacuation plan
summary: Plan assistance before a lift outage or evacuation so a person with limited mobility is not left to an unsafe improvised carry.
content:
Plan assistance before a lift outage or evacuation so a person with limited mobility is not left to an unsafe improvised carry.

Field sequence: Agree on trained helpers, communication, safe waiting area, mobility aids, medical equipment, route width, handover point, and when emergency services must perform the movement. Confirm the reason for movement, official instruction, destination, primary route, fallback route, weather, daylight, and transport limits for “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan”. Mark hazards such as fire, smoke, floodwater, damaged bridges, unstable slopes, crowds, poor visibility, and restricted areas for “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan”. Match pace, load, rest, assistance, and transport to children, older adults, disability, illness, animals, and the slowest safe member for “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan”. Leave a departure time, route, destination, group list, check-in schedule, and missed-contact rule with a trusted person for “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan”. Stop at planned checkpoints, compare real conditions with the map, and turn back or divert before the route becomes trapping or irreversible for “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan”. Safety limits: Official instructions and emergency services override “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan”; do not delay evacuation or urgent help to complete the card. Never enter moving floodwater, wildfire closure, smoke-obscured road, unstable slope, damaged structure, or officially restricted route in “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan”. Do not split the group without a written destination, communication rule, responsible adults, and reunification plan in “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan”. Reduced fallback: If “paper map and pencil for “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan”” is unavailable, use only a clean, labelled substitute that performs the same function; otherwise mark the missing capability and pause that step.
materials: paper map and pencil for “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan”
official alert and route information for “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan”
contact and meeting-point card for “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan”
light, water, and essential medicine for “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan”
mobility and visibility aids for “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan”
steps: Agree on trained helpers, communication, safe waiting area, mobility aids, medical equipment, route width, handover point, and when emergency services must perform the movement.
Confirm the reason for movement, official instruction, destination, primary route, fallback route, weather, daylight, and transport limits for “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan”.
Mark hazards such as fire, smoke, floodwater, damaged bridges, unstable slopes, crowds, poor visibility, and restricted areas for “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan”.
Match pace, load, rest, assistance, and transport to children, older adults, disability, illness, animals, and the slowest safe member for “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan”.
Leave a departure time, route, destination, group list, check-in schedule, and missed-contact rule with a trusted person for “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan”.
Stop at planned checkpoints, compare real conditions with the map, and turn back or divert before the route becomes trapping or irreversible for “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan”.
warnings: Official instructions and emergency services override “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan”; do not delay evacuation or urgent help to complete the card.
Never enter moving floodwater, wildfire closure, smoke-obscured road, unstable slope, damaged structure, or officially restricted route in “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan”.
Do not split the group without a written destination, communication rule, responsible adults, and reunification plan in “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan”.
common_mistakes: Skipping the opening control makes “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan” unreliable: Agree on trained helpers, communication, safe waiting area, mobility aids, medical equipment, route width, handover point, and when emergency services must perform the movement.

Treating the result as safe while ignoring this limit is a serious error: Official instructions and emergency services override “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan”; do not delay evacuation or urgent help to complete the card.

Failing to document the final handover action leaves the next person without a trustworthy starting point: Stop at planned checkpoints, compare real conditions with the map, and turn back or divert before the route becomes trapping or irreversible for “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan”.
alternatives: If “paper map and pencil for “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan”” is unavailable, use only a clean, labelled substitute that performs the same function; otherwise mark the missing capability and pause that step.

For a reduced version of “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan”, complete the first two verified actions—Agree on trained helpers, communication, safe waiting area, mobility aids, medical equipment, route width, handover point, and when emergency services must perform the movement. Confirm the reason for movement, official instruction, destination, primary route, fallback route, weather, daylight, and transport limits for “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan”.—then stop at the first uncertainty and request appropriate help.
failure_signs: Stop and reassess “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan” when the route loses a safe exit, visibility, communications, daylight margin, water margin, or physical capacity.
Stop and reassess “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan” when a member is missing, distressed, injured, exhausted, confused, or unable to continue safely.
when_not_to_use: Do not continue “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan” while breathing, alertness, circulation, major bleeding, severe pain, seizure, poisoning, chest symptoms, pregnancy danger signs, or rapid deterioration is present.

This card is not authorization to diagnose, prescribe, change a dose, perform an invasive procedure, force care, or delay 112 and qualified medical help. Topic-specific stop check for “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan”: also follow this warning: “Official instructions and emergency services override “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan”; do not delay evacuation or urgent help to complete the card.”.
short_term: For the first operating cycle of “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan”, carry out agree on trained helpers, communication, safe waiting area, mobility aids, medical equipment, route width, handover point, and when emergency services must perform the movement. Follow with confirm the reason for movement, official instruction, destination, primary route, fallback route, weather, daylight, and transport limits for “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan”. Before repeating the procedure, verify that this failure sign is absent: Stop and reassess “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan” when the route loses a safe exit, visibility, communications, daylight margin, water margin, or physical capacity.
long_term: For repeated use of “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan”, keep “official alert and route information for “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan”” serviceable, rehearse the handover described in the final step, compare records with real outcomes, and revise the card whenever local conditions or official instructions change.
if_method_fails: If “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan” does not produce a verifiable result, stop using that result, label or isolate anything uncertain, protect people from the identified failure path, record what happened, and change to the safest official, professionally supported, or previously tested fallback.
environment_notes: Use consent, privacy, infection-control barriers, the person’s established care plan, written medicines and allergies, and early referral when transport is difficult. For “Mobility-support stair evacuation plan”, also account for the people, animals, tools, transport, communications, and qualified help that are actually available at the location.
